SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.corrosion

Class RobustAqueousPH

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.corrosion.RobustAqueousPH
public final class RobustAqueousPH extends Object
Robust in-situ aqueous pH estimator for corrosion and scaling investigations.

The rigorous electrolyte in-situ pH from SystemInterface.getpH() is the preferred value, but it is not robust across all pressure and temperature conditions (for example it can return NaN at low pressure, and it can return an unphysical basic value if the chemical-reaction equilibrium was not solved). An investigation that blindly consumes that value can silently propagate a wrong pH into a corrosion or scale calculation.

This helper returns a value that is always finite and physically bounded: it takes the electrolyte pH when that value is finite and inside a plausible band, and otherwise falls back to a CO2-water carbonic-acid correlation (the same form used by NorsokM506CorrosionRate). The result records which source was used so the caller can flag fallbacks in a report.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL
See Also:
  • NorsokM506CorrosionRate

  • Constructor Details

    • RobustAqueousPH

      private RobustAqueousPH()

  • Method Details

    • estimate

      public static RobustAqueousPH.Result estimate(SystemInterface fluid, double co2PartialPressureBar)
      Estimates a robust in-situ aqueous pH from an electrolyte fluid, falling back to a CO2-water correlation.
      Parameters:
      fluid - a flashed electrolyte fluid (should have an aqueous phase); must not be null
      co2PartialPressureBar - the CO2 partial pressure in bar, used only for the correlation fallback
      Returns:
      a finite, physically bounded pH result

    • correlationPH

      public static double correlationPH(double temperatureC, double co2PartialPressureBar)
      CO2-water carbonic-acid pH correlation used as the robust fallback.

      Uses the combined temperature-dependent function pH = 0.5 * (pKa1' + pKH) - 0.5 * log10(fCO2), consistent with NorsokM506CorrosionRate.calculateEquilibriumPH().

      Parameters:
      temperatureC - temperature in degrees Celsius
      co2PartialPressureBar - CO2 partial pressure in bar
      Returns:
      the correlated CO2-water pH (clamped to the plausible band)
